items.mistake: Applying ReLU on the output layer of a multi-class classifier
items.fix: Use raw logits with `CrossEntropyLoss` or apply softmax only at inference; keep the final linear layer without ReLU.
items.why: ReLU clips negative logits to zero, destroying class scores and making correct classification impossible for many labels.
items.example: 10-class head: `nn.Linear(64, 10)` → ReLU → loss never learns class 3 if its logit must be negative relative to others.

items.mistake: Ignoring a high fraction of dead ReLU neurons during training
items.fix: Log per-layer activation sparsity; try Leaky ReLU, lower learning rate, He initialization, or batch normalization.
items.why: Many dead neurons reduce model capacity silently—loss may plateau while the network effectively shrinks.
items.example: If 40% of hidden units never fire on a training batch, switch `nn.ReLU()` to `nn.LeakyReLU(0.01)` and compare dead-neuron counts.

items.mistake: Confusing Leaky ReLU α with PReLU learnable weights
items.fix: Set `negative_slope` explicitly for Leaky ReLU; use `nn.PReLU(num_parameters=channels)` when α should be learned per channel.
items.why: Leaky α is a fixed hyperparameter; PReLU adds trainable parameters that need optimizer steps and correct shape.
items.example: `nn.LeakyReLU(0.01)` has no extra params; `nn.PReLU(128)` adds 128 learnable α values updated via backprop.

items.mistake: Expecting ReLU alone to fix all gradient pathologies
items.fix: Combine ReLU with sensible initialization, normalization, learning-rate tuning, and gradient clipping where needed.
items.why: ReLU removes sigmoid saturation but dead neurons and large weights can still block or explode gradient flow.
items.example: 10-layer MLP with ReLU but lr=1.0 may diverge; ReLU does not replace learning-rate or initialization discipline.
